name: claude-code-headless
description: Run Claude Code programmatically without interactive UI. Triggers on: headless, CLI automation, --print, output-format, stream-json, CI/CD, scripting.
capabilities: Run Claude Code from shell scripts without interactive prompts
Generate structured JSON output for programmatic parsing
Control tool access with --allowedTools and --disallowedTools flags
Resume conversations using session IDs for multi-turn workflows
Integrate with CI/CD systems like GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Stream real-time JSON output for live progress tracking
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files that document Claude Code CLI usage. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES because the 'external_commands' patterns are documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not actual executable code. The skill contains no scripts, no file operations, no network calls, and no credential access. Shell constructs like $(...) are examples showing how to parse Claude Code JSON output, not malicious command execution.
valueStatement: Claude Code headless mode enables programmatic execution from shell scripts and CI/CD pipelines. This skill provides documentation and examples for non-interactive automation with structured JSON output.
userTitle: Run Claude Code in headless CLI mode
limitations: Requires Claude Code CLI installation on the system
Needs valid ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for API access
Cannot modify system prompt beyond append/prepend options
Limited to tools and permissions configured at runtime

useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Review
useCases.description: Automate code review in GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins pipelines

useCases.title: Scripted Automation
useCases.description: Create shell scripts for batch processing and repeated tasks

useCases.title: Automated Auditing
useCases.description: Run security scans programmatically with restricted tool access

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Turn Session
promptTemplates.prompt: claude -p "Start analysis" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id' then claude --resume "$session" "Continue analysis"

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: claude -p "Review PR diff" --permission-mode acceptEdits --output-format json --append-system-prompt "Focus on security"

bestPractices: Use --output-format json for reliable programmatic parsing in scripts
Restrict tool access with --allowedTools and --disallowedTools for security
Handle session IDs and errors explicitly for robust automation
antiPatterns: Using headless mode without tool restrictions in untrusted contexts
Ignoring exit codes and error responses in automation scripts
Hardcoding API keys in scripts instead of using environment variables

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill documents Claude Code CLI headless mode for claude-code tool. It enables automation through command-line flags.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for headless mode?
faq.answer: Headless mode uses standard Claude API rate limits. Check your Anthropic API plan for specific quotas.

faq.question: How do I integrate with CI/CD systems?
faq.answer: Use --output-format json and --permission-mode acceptEdits flags. Examples for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Jenkins are provided.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using headless mode?
faq.answer: Yes. Data handling follows Claude Code standard policies. Use --allowedTools to restrict access to sensitive resources.

faq.question: Why is my headless command blocking?
faq.answer: Default permission mode requires prompts. Use --permission-mode bypassPermissions or --permission-mode acceptEdits for non-interactive execution.

faq.question: How does this compare to interactive mode?
faq.answer: Headless mode provides CLI access for scripting with structured JSON output. Interactive mode offers chat interface with real-time responses.
